_comment: TRUE POSITIVE — SP-103001: LSASS memory dump via procdump (T1003.001)
scenario: Attacker uses procdump to dump LSASS memory and extract credentials for lateral movement
why_tp: procdump64.exe accessing LSASS with PROCESS_VM_READ (0x1410) — textbook credential dumping
Tool written to C:\Temp — attacker staging location
AccessMask 0x1410 = PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION + PROCESS_VM_READ — the minimum for a memory dump
Sysmon event 10 (ProcessAccess) corroborates the 4656 — dual-source confirmation
analyst_action: IMMEDIATE: Check if any lateral movement occurred from WORKSTATION-SALES-03 after 15:33
Identify all accounts whose credentials may have been in LSASS (logged-in users)
Force password reset for all accounts found on this machine
Investigate how procdump64.exe arrived on the host (parent process, download source)
